statusLine: EU fuel reserves remain under strain despite safe average levels, with 15 of 27 countries experiencing critical shortages across different fuel types amid elevated oil prices.
fullAnalysis:
European fuel security shows a concerning divergence between overall averages and individual country vulnerabilities. While EU-wide averages exceed the 90-day minimum requirement (petrol 113 days, diesel 102.1 days), the distribution reveals significant weaknesses. Romania faces the most severe pressure with all three fuel types in critical status, including diesel reserves at just 30.2 days. Poland and Luxembourg also show critical levels across all categories, while major economies like Germany and Italy struggle with diesel shortages despite adequate petrol stocks.

The jet fuel situation presents particular challenges, with 15 of 27 countries at critical levels. This affects major aviation hubs including Germany (64.7 days) and France (67.5 days), potentially impacting European connectivity. Diesel reserves show widespread pressure, with 13 countries below safe levels, including Spain and several Eastern European nations that serve as key transit routes.

Oil market conditions compound these supply pressures. Brent crude has surged 28% since late February to $92.78/barrel, reflecting ongoing Middle East disruptions. This price elevation, combined with the strategic reserve releases several countries have already implemented, suggests limited buffer capacity for further supply shocks. The wide price variation across member states, from Malta's €1.34/L to Denmark's €2.50/L for petrol, indicates uneven market access and domestic policy responses.
keyPoints: 15 of 27 EU countries face critical jet fuel shortages, including major economies Germany (64.7 days) and France (67.5 days)
Romania experiences the most severe crisis with all fuel types critical, including diesel at just 30.2 days versus the 90-day minimum
13 of 27 countries show diesel reserves below safe levels, affecting key transit nations Spain, Italy, and Poland
Brent crude has surged 28% since late February to $92.78/barrel, limiting strategic reserve release options
Fuel prices vary dramatically across the bloc, with petrol ranging from €1.34/L in Malta to €2.50/L in Denmark
